sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, created_at TIMESTAMP, value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, created_at, value) VALUES (1, TIMESTAMP '2023-10-01 12:00:00', 'Sample Value 1'), (2, TIMESTAMP '2023-09-30 12:00:00', NULL), (3, TIMESTAMP '2023-10-02 12:00:00', '');
SELECT id, dayofmonth(created_at) || ': ' || value AS day_value FROM test_table WHERE created_at IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE join_table (join_id INT PRIMARY KEY, test_id INT, FOREIGN KEY (test_id) REFERENCES test_table(id));
INSERT INTO join_table (join_id, test_id) VALUES (1, 1), (2, NULL), (3, 3);
SELECT jt.join_id, dayofmonth(tt.created_at) || ' - ' || jt.test_id AS detailed_info FROM join_table jt JOIN test_table tt ON jt.test_id = tt.id OR jt.test_id IS NULL;
SELECT jt.join_id, COUNT(*) AS count_days FROM join_table jt GROUP BY dayofmonth(tt.created_at) HAVING COUNT(*) > 1;
SELECT id, (dayofmonth(created_at) + 1) || ' - ' || value AS updated_value FROM test_table ORDER BY updated_value DESC;
SELECT id, CASE WHEN value IS NULL THEN 'No Value' ELSE value END || ' - Day: ' || dayofmonth(created_at) AS case_value FROM test_table;
SELECT id, (dayofmonth(created_at) || ' - ' || COALESCE(value, 'Empty')) AS coalesce_value FROM test_table WHERE created_at BETWEEN TIMESTAMP '2023-09-01' AND TIMESTAMP '2023-10-31';
